evals.prompt: How do I update evals when my code changes?
evals.expected_output: The skill loads the plugin README, anchors on the 'Updating evals when code changes' section, reads the host repo's manifest.yml to tailor the answer (e.g. names a real target or notes its absence), and composes a paraphrased response with at least one start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md citation that includes the _meta.generated contract, --check CI mode, and targeted-vs-rediscovery modes. The skill does not call askQuestion.
assertions: The skill read plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md
The skill attempted to read .zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml (and stated its presence or absence)
The response paraphrases the 'Updating evals when code changes' section rather than pasting it whole
The response includes at least one fenced code block whose info string matches the start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md code-reference syntax
The cited README lines actually cover the _meta.generated contract, --check CI mode, and targeted vs rediscovery modes
The response references a project-specific value derived from manifest.yml (e.g. a target id, target count) OR clearly states the manifest is absent and points to /z-eval-create
The skill did not call askQuestion

evals.expected_output: The /z-eval-help command (not the skill) opens a numbered askQuestion listing of all README ## sections. The skill is only invoked after a section is chosen; until then no section content or tailored answer is rendered.
assertions: The command (not the skill) used askQuestion with a numbered list of sections
The list includes at least: Overview, Quick start, Configuration, LLM backend (@cursor/sdk), Updating evals when code changes, Comparing runs, CI integration, Troubleshooting
No section body, paraphrase, or citation block was emitted before the user selected a section
The skill itself did not call askQuestion

evals.prompt: I just ran /z-eval-create. What does my LLM backend configuration actually look like and where do I put my CURSOR_API_KEY?
evals.expected_output: The skill anchors on the 'LLM backend (@cursor/sdk)' section, reads .zoto/eval-system/config.yml to report the user's actual llm.runtime / llm.model.id / llm.strategy values, checks whether .env.example and .env exist (without reading secret values from .env), and produces a tailored answer with start:end citations to the README's LLM backend lines and to the config.json field values. Falls back to needs_user_input only if selected_section is missing.
assertions: The skill read plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md and anchored on the 'LLM backend (@cursor/sdk)' section
The skill read .zoto/eval-system/config.yml and named the user's actual llm.runtime, llm.model.id, and (when present) llm.strategy values
The skill checked the presence of .env.example and .env without echoing any secret values from .env
The response includes at least one start:end:plugins/zoto-eval-system/README.md citation covering the .env / CURSOR_API_KEY onboarding steps
The response paraphrases rather than dumping the full LLM backend section verbatim
If config.json is absent, the skill says so and points to /z-eval-configure instead of inventing values
The skill did not call askQuestion
